revision.text:
'''{{nihongo|Nagata Shrine|長田神社,|Nagata-jinja}}''' is a [[Shinto]] shrine in Nagata Ward, [[Kobe]], [[Japan]].
[[Category:Shinto shrines]]

'''{{nihongo|Nagata Shrine|長田神社,|Nagata-jinja}}''' is a [[Shinto]] shrine in Nagata Ward, [[Kobe]], [[Japan]].
According to ''Nihonshoki'', Nagata Jinja was founded by the [[Jingu of Japan|Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the [[3rd century]] CE along with [[Hirota Jinja]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Jinja]], dedicated to the [[kami]] Wakahirume.
[[Category:Shinto shrines]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nagata jinja
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | type = | dedication = | founded = 201 | closed = | founder = Empress Jingū | priest = | address = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | phone = 078-691-0333 | website = http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata jinja'''|長田神社 |}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in Nagata Ward, [[Kobe]], [[Japan]].Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by the [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for yjr ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==Related shrines== Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{fact}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1030156 OCLC 1030156] {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{ja}} {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments]] [[Category:Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nagata jinja
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | type = | dedication = | founded = 201 | closed = | founder = Empress Jingū | priest = | address = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | phone = 078-691-0333 | website = http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata jinja'''|長田神社 |}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in Nagata Ward, [[Kobe]], [[Japan]].Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by the [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for yjr ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==Related shrines== Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1030156 OCLC 1030156] {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{ja}} {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments]] [[Category:Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nagata jinja
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | type = | dedication = | founded = 201 | closed = | founder = Empress Jingū | priest = | address = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | phone = 078-691-0333 | website = http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata jinja'''|長田神社 |}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in Nagata Ward, [[Kobe]], [[Japan]].Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by the [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Takebe was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for yjr ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==Related shrines== Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1030156 OCLC 1030156] {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{ja}} {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments]] [[Category:Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]]

From 1871 through 1946, the Takebe was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for yjr ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==Related shrines== Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}}[[Category:Beppo Shrines]] {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.Kobe Convention and Visitors Association, [http://feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 Nagata Jinja Shrine] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |date=2016-06-23 }} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p, 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-chūsha]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Moving [[:Category:Kanpei-chūsha]] to [[:Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Speedy]]

The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Nagata is related to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2011}} ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. 長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]].
