{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[:en:En'nichi|En'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Nagata]] [[fr:Nagata-jinja]] [[id:Nagata jinja]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]] [[zh:長田神社]]

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[:en:En'nichi|En'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Nagata]] [[fr:Nagata-jinja]] [[id:Nagata jinja]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]] [[zh:長田神社]]

Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Nagata]] [[fr:Nagata-jinja]] [[id:Nagata jinja]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]] [[zh:長田神社]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q661395]] Wikidata name: Nagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[:en:En'nichi|En'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagata Shrine]] [[en:Nagata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} [[de:Nagata-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Nagata]] [[fr:Nagata-jinja]] [[id:Nagata jinja]] [[it:Santuario Nagata]] [[ja:長田神社]] [[zh:長田神社]]

At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[:en:En'nichi|En'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. revision.text:
{{distinguish|Nagata Shrine (Q135069149)}} '''Nagata Shrine (Q661395)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Yatabe district|ja|八部郡|zh|八部郡|WD=Q11391769}} || {{ill|Nagata Shrine|lt=Nagata Shrine|en|Nagata Shrine|ja|長田神社|commons|Category:Nagata-jinja|de|Nagata-Schrein|es|Santuario Nagata|fr|Nagata-jinja|id|Nagata jinja|it|Santuario Nagata|zh|長田神社|WD=Q661395}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|WD=Q135009157|lt=Ainame}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|WD=Q11581944}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || — || — || {{coord|34.671200|135.146995|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181075 DB] |} ==Overview== {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Images]]

At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]

From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. 長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]

Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Yatabe district|ja|八部郡|zh|八部郡|WD=Q11391769}} || {{ill|Nagata Shrine|lt=Nagata Shrine|en|Nagata Shrine|ja|長田神社|commons|Category:Nagata-jinja|de|Nagata-Schrein|es|Santuario Nagata|fr|Nagata-jinja|id|Nagata jinja|it|Santuario Nagata|zh|長田神社|WD=Q661395}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|WD=Q135009157|lt=Ainame}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|WD=Q11581944}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || — || — || {{coord|34.671200|135.146995|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181075 DB] |} ==Overview== {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nagata-jinja]][[de:Nagata-Schrein]][[en:Nagata Shrine]][[es:Santuario Nagata]][[fr:Nagata-jinja]][[id:Nagata jinja]][[it:Santuario Nagata]][[ja:長田神社]][[zh:長田神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nagata-jinja]][[de:Nagata-Schrein]][[en:Nagata Shrine]][[es:Santuario Nagata]][[fr:Nagata-jinja]][[id:Nagata jinja]][[it:Santuario Nagata]][[ja:長田神社]][[zh:長田神社]] {{distinguish|Nagata Shrine (Q135069149)}} '''Nagata Shrine (Q661395)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Yatabe district|ja|八部郡|zh|八部郡|WD=Q11391769}} || {{ill|Nagata Shrine|lt=Nagata Shrine|en|Nagata Shrine|ja|長田神社|commons|Category:Nagata-jinja|de|Nagata-Schrein|es|Santuario Nagata|fr|Nagata-jinja|id|Nagata jinja|it|Santuario Nagata|zh|長田神社|WD=Q661395}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|WD=Q135009157|lt=Ainame}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|WD=Q11581944}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || — || — || {{coord|34.671200|135.146995|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181075 DB] |} ==Overview== {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

(1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nagata-jinja]][[de:Nagata-Schrein]][[en:Nagata Shrine]][[es:Santuario Nagata]][[fr:Nagata-jinja]][[id:Nagata jinja]][[it:Santuario Nagata]][[ja:長田神社]][[zh:長田神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nagata-jinja]][[de:Nagata-Schrein]][[en:Nagata Shrine]][[es:Santuario Nagata]][[fr:Nagata-jinja]][[id:Nagata jinja]][[it:Santuario Nagata]][[ja:長田神社]][[zh:長田神社]] {{distinguish|Nagata Shrine (Q135069149)}} '''Nagata Shrine (Q661395)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Yatabe district|ja|八部郡|zh|八部郡|qid=Q11391769|1=Yatabe district}} || {{ill|Nagata Shrine|lt=Nagata Shrine|en|Nagata Shrine|ja|長田神社|commons|Category:Nagata-jinja|de|Nagata-Schrein|es|Santuario Nagata|fr|Nagata-jinja|id|Nagata jinja|it|Santuario Nagata|zh|長田神社|qid=Q661395|1=Nagata Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|qid=Q135009157|lt=Ainame|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|qid=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.671200|135.146995|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181075 DB] |} ==Overview== {{short description|Shinto shrine in Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Nagata Shrine
長田神社 | image = Nagata-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Nagata jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|17|N|135|08|49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Kotoshironushi]] | founded_by = Empress Jingū | established = 201 | date_destroyed = | location = Kobe, Nagata-ku Nagata-cho 3-1-1 | website = {{URL|http://nagatajinja.jp/html/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagata Shrine'''|長田神社|Nagata jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Nagata-ku, Kobe]], [[Japan]].Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 324-328. At Nagata, Kotoshironushi-no-Okami is enshrined.{{cite web |title=Nagata Shrine |url=http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |website=Feel Kobe |publisher=Kobe Convention and Visitors Association |access-date=2023-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623205316/http://www.feel-kobe.jp/_en/sightseeing/spot/?sid=124 |archive-date=2016-06-23 |url-status=dead}} The shrine is associated with [[Amaterasu]], who is said to have told [[Empress Jingū]] that a shrine was wanted at Nagata.Ponsonby-Fane, p. 321. ==History== According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nagata was founded by [[Empress Jingū]] at the beginning of the 3rd century along with [[Hirota Shrine]]. In 2001, the shrine celebrated its 1,800 years of history. From 1871 through 1946, the Nagata was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''kanpei-chūsha'']]|官幣中社}},Ponsonby-Fane, p. 322. meaning that it stood in the second tier of government supported shrines which were especially venerated by the imperial family. ==Festivals and events == An autumn matsuri in October is a special day (''[[en'nichi]]'') for the ''kami'' Kotoshironushi.Librarie Sansaisha. (1907). "''L'anéee 1907 au japon d'aprés l'ancien et le nouveau calendrier''," in {{Google books|ZtsqAAAAYAAJ|''Mélanges japonais''. Vol. 4, No 13, p. 122.|page=122|language=fr}}; Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=769 ''En'nichi'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 A ''[[setsubun]]'' observance in February is the ''Tsuina-shiki Shinji,'' which engages hopes for safety in the home and averting misfortune.Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=904 ''Tsuina shiki'']; [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=324 ''Engimono'']; retrieved 2011-08-19 This Shinto purification ritual is designated as an [[intangible cultural heritage]] event. The elaborate ceremony is a pantomime representation of driving out demons or bad spirits.Ponsonby-Fane, pp. 327-328. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Twenty-Two Shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * ''[[Nagata Maru]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagata-jinja}} * [http://nagatajinja.jp/html/ Nagata jinja official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Hyogo-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-shrine-stub}} {{wikidata link|Q661395}} [[Category:3rd-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kobe]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
